title: Kentwood Nightstand
summary: A frame-and-panel nightstand with wainscoting panel inserts for the sides and door — built from a 1x2/1x3/1x4 stick frame, not a solid plywood box, so the panels can float and show real texture.
description:
This nightstand is built frame-and-panel, not as a plywood cabinet — 1x2 and 1x4 rails form the top and bottom of each side frame, joined by 1x2 verticals, with a thin wainscoting panel let into the frame rather than glued flat to a plywood face. That's what gives the sides real dimensional texture instead of a flat painted surface, and it's a different construction method from this catalog's other enclosed nightstand, which is a solid plywood box.

The door is built the same way — a 1x3 frame around its own wainscoting panel insert, sized to close over the lower storage compartment. Hang it on a pair of 2" strap hinges, which suit the exposed-frame look better than concealed cabinet hinges would, and add a magnetic clasp to keep it shut.

A plywood shelf sits inside at the drawer/storage divide, with an optional second shelf cut from the same strip if extra storage is wanted. Optional 1/4" plywood can close in the back if you don't want the wainscoting texture visible from behind.

materials.name: Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in
Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two side frame top/bottom rails, narrow at 12-3/4", four side frame verticals at 26-1/4" and two front frame rails, narrow at 17" from the 1x2. Cut two side frame top/bottom rails, wide at 12-3/4", two front frame rails, wide at 17" and four front verticals at 22-1/2" from the 1x4. Cut two side panels at 21-3/4" from the Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in. Cut one shelf at 20-1/2" from the 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips. Cut four door frame verticals at 26-1/4", two door rails at 16-3/4" and two door stiles at 14" from the 1x3. Cut one door panel at 19" from the Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x2, two 8 ft 1x4 and two 8 ft 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.
materials: Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in

steps.title: Build the side frames
steps.body: Assemble the side frame rails and verticals, then let the wainscoting side panels into each frame.
materials: Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in

steps.title: Build the front frame
steps.body: Assemble the front frame rails and verticals, sized to accept the door.

steps.title: Join the frames and add the shelf
steps.body: Join the side frames to the front frame, then set the shelf in place at the storage divide.

steps.title: Build and hang the door
steps.body: Build the door frame around its wainscoting panel, then hang with the strap hinges and add the magnetic clasp.
materials: Pine wainscoting panel, 48 in x 22 in

steps.title: Add hardware and finish
steps.body: Attach the knob, add the optional plywood back if desired, sand, and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Kentwood frame and panel nightstand with wainscoting door
